body: Taiko's tonight — my inbound lands 4:10 so it's tight for school pickup. Can you grab Hana and Kenji? Don't want to miss the beginner session again.
I've got them. Go straight to class, don't stress it.
Yosh. Thank you. Kenji's been asking if he can watch sometime — maybe next week?
He's been talking about the drums nonstop lol. I'll bring him. 🥁
Heads up — taiko runs late tonight so I won't make pickup. Mom's got it. You still want to come watch one of these Tuesdays or are you still on the 'it's embarrassing' stance? 😄
Dad you're literally a PILOT why are you playing drums 💀 but ok fine I'll come once. ONCE.
Yosh. I'll take it. Bring Kenji if he's not being dramatic. Bellevue Community Center, 7pm — wear something you can actually move in.
Kenji is ALWAYS being dramatic but fine. Will mom do matcha before we go? 🍵
Dad are you actually doing taiko tomorrow night or are you gonna bail again like two weeks ago lol
I didn't bail. Bellevue to Seattle in that rain wasn't happening in time. Tuesday I'm going — already blocked the calendar.
can me and Hana come watch sometime? she thinks taiko is just loud drumming
It is loud drumming. That's the point. Yosh — next time I'll bring you both. Tell your mom so she knows pickup's on her.
Hana called me after school — she said little Kenji told his whole class his dad plays taiko 😄 You're famous in Bellevue now.
Beginner taiko. Let's not get ahead of ourselves. I can barely keep the tempo on the don-ko pattern.
Your ojiichan would have loved this. He always said taiko was the heartbeat of the house. Tomorrow again?
Hai, after school pickup. Kenji wants to come watch one day — I'll wait until I stop dropping the beat before I let that happen.
yo are you seriously still doing that beginner taiko class 😂 i thought that was a two-week thing
still going. tuesday nights. three months in and sensei has me on the same warm-up pattern every week — fairly sure she's testing something.
lmao a 737 first officer doing beginner taiko in bellevue. hana and kenji think it's cool at least?
kenji wants to come watch. hana said it's embarrassing. split decision. yosh, back to it.
ok wait did you actually go to taiko again last night 😂 I need proof you're not just telling mom you have a hobby
I went. Two hours, blisters on both hands. Sensei said my timing is 'improving.' Felt like a polite lie but I'll take it.
lmaooo 'improving' is definitely pilot-speak for disaster. does Aisha know you're out here suffering every Tuesday night in Bellevue
She knows. She and Hana think it's very funny. yosh — at least someone in this family believes in me.
Hey Patricia — Aisha's got a PT appointment running late and I push back at 2:40 out of SEA. Any chance you can grab Hana from school today? Kenji's covered, he's got after-school till 5.
Of course! What time does she get out? And should I bring her to yours or take her back to mine?
3:15, Bellevue Elementary. Take her to ours — spare key's in the planter. Aisha should be home by 5:30. Really appreciate it, Patricia 🙏
Already heading out! Tell Aisha not to stress. Hana and I will figure out a snack situation 😄
Kenji called me last night, said you've got some drumming class on Tuesdays now 😂 a 737 pilot doing beginner taiko in Bellevue. Aisha know what she signed up for?
It's taiko, not drumming class. And yeah she knows — she's the one who found it. Kenji came once and stood in the back looking mortified the whole time.
Ha. He told me you were actually decent though. Didn't want to admit it but it slipped out.
Yosh. Don't tell him I know that — I need the leverage. Class is tomorrow, 7pm. He's welcome to come look mortified again.

Taiko last night almost ended me. Sensei had us drilling the same 8-beat phrase for 45 straight minutes — my forearms are wrecked on top of the 5am Equinox session this morning. Beginner is not a metaphor.
Gambatte. You chose this, which counts for something. How's it sitting compared to type-rating training?
Type rating was hard but I had a framework. This is just me in a room of 22-year-olds who've never flown anything and they're still beating me. Humbling in a way the 737 sim never is.
That's exactly why you're doing it. See you on the Bellevue turnaround Thursday — bring the forearms.
Daichi — Kenji still good for Tuesday? I saved him a spot next to yours, figured he'd like being paired with his dad for the kata intro.
He's been asking about it all week honestly. Aisha's doing pickup so we'll come straight from Bellevue, should be there by 7:05.
Perfect, bachi are prepped. Fair warning — beginners get the odaiko for the opening exercise. He's gonna love it 😄
Yosh. That'll either hook him for life or shake him off the idea entirely. Gambatte to us both.
Heard you finally started that taiko class in Bellevue — how's it going? My nephew did it for a year and said the first two months are genuinely humbling 😄
Ha. Humbling's the word. Tuesdays, beginner session, I'm the only one there who's not 15. Sensei's patient though. Good reset after a line week on the MAX.
Nice. You still flying the SEA routes mostly? Tell Aisha I said hi — feel like we haven't crossed paths since that thing at the club back in March.
Yeah, SEA and a Phoenix rotation. Will pass it on. Hana just wrapped second grade somehow — come by sometime, Kenji will absolutely make you sit through his entire Lego presentation.
Hey Rachel — Hana's been at 101.2 since last night. No cough, no rash, just off her food. She's 8. Worth bringing her in or give it another day?
101.2 with no other symptoms is usually day one of something viral. Push fluids, check again tonight. If it climbs to 103 or she develops a sore throat, text me.
Copy. She asked for matcha at breakfast so I'm cautiously optimistic. I'm on a PDX turn today — back in Bellevue by 6, Aisha's got her until then.
If she's requesting matcha she's probably going to be fine. 😄 Keep an eye on it and let me know tonight if anything shifts.

Meredith — Hana's been running the Vivaldi A minor every morning before school. She had the second movement locked cold during my 6am matcha today. Is she close to performance-ready for the July showcase?
Yes! I was literally about to text you. Something clicked for her this month. The showcase is July 19th at Bellevue Arts Center — I've been holding a spot for her.
Yosh. I'm on a Ridgecrest rotation the 17th–18th but I'll make the 19th no problem. Aisha's confirmed for pickup Thursday either way.
Perfect. Just have her drill measures 26–30 this week, that's the only rough edge left. She's really got this 😊
Hey Carlos — Daichi Mosley, Kenji's dad. I'm on a SEA-PHX leg Tuesday so Aisha's covering pickup. Practice still wrapping at 5:30?
5:30 on the dot. Tell her to park on the Bellevue High side — main lot gets gridlocked after 5.
Got it, I'll pass that along. Kenji's been working his left foot in the backyard all week, curious if you're seeing any of it carry over.
Actually yeah — Thursday he tracked a ball on his weak side that surprised me. Kid's putting in real work.
Robert — sent over the 2025 per diem log last night. 187 away days total, mix of SEA, LAX, DEN layovers. Let me know if you need the Ridgecrest trip sheets broken out by month.
Got it, thanks. Were any of those international? And did Aisha have any 1099 income this year we should capture?
All domestic. Aisha's W-2 only, same employer. Also — we added Kenji to the FSA this year, he started kindergarten in September.
Perfect. I'll run the dependent care credit against the FSA contributions and flag whichever comes out ahead. Draft back to you by Friday.
Daichi — Q2 just closed and Hana's 529 is sitting at 68% equity. With your Ridgecrest schedule picking up this summer, want to bump contributions before the July statement?
Yosh. How much are we short of the annual max? Aisha and I were actually talking about this last week.

You're $2,400 under the annual limit. I can queue a one-time transfer from your joint account — just need the green light. WA has no state income tax so no deduction to chase, but the compounding window on Hana's timeline is solid.
Go ahead and queue it. Send the confirmation when it posts — I'm on a Bellevue–LAX turn tomorrow and back by dinner.
Hey man somebody just told me you're doing beginner taiko on Tuesdays now 😂 in Bellevue? That's a new one for a 737 FO. Please tell me Aisha dragged you into this.
Yosh. Eight months in, actually. Hana's been asking to join the kids' session in the fall — so now it's a whole family situation apparently.
Ok I respect it more than I should lol. Does hammering a drum mess with your grip at all? Asking for... reasons.
Hasn't come up on any Ridgecrest leg yet. See you on the pairing Thursday — I'll report back if I can't hold the yoke 🙄
Daichi-san, you coming to class tomorrow? Sensei said we're finally drilling the don-don-ko pattern and honestly I've been dreading it all week 😅
Hai, I'll be there. Picking up Hana first so I'll land at the studio right at 7. Don-don-ko is just subdivisions — your hands already know how to do this.
Easy for you to say 😂 also weren't you on a flight last night? How are you not exhausted rn
Bellevue to Bellevue, home by 10. Slept fine. Gambatte — slow the pattern down to nothing first, then build. See you tomorrow.
Hey saw you heading out last night with what looked like a drum bag?? Is that the taiko class you mentioned??
Yosh. Every Tuesday. Beginner level — turns out flying a 737 does nothing to prepare you for taiko timing. Completely different skill set.
Ha! Are spots still open? Sounds like something I could embarrass myself at in Bellevue on a Tuesday night 😂
Plenty of room. Show up at 7, bring water. Warn them you're with me so they lower expectations upfront.
Hey, Takeshi said you're doing beginner taiko on Tuesdays now?? Is it actually beginner or is he underselling it like he does with every hike 😅
Legit beginner. I'm the guy who counts out loud. Instructor's patient. You should drag him — he needs a hobby that isn't watching Bellevue traffic reports.
Ha, he really does. Maybe I come solo first and scout it. Is it the studio off 108th or somewhere else downtown?
Yosh, 108th. 7pm sharp — pilot thing, I hate late arrivals. Text me before and I'll save you a spot in the back where the mistakes are less visible.
